summary.competitive_position: VeriClaw has the smallest container image (37.1 MB vs 42-48 MB). Binary size (5.3 MB) beats zeroclaw (8.8 MB) but trails nullclaw/Zig (0.66 MB). Latency/throughput not directly comparable due to QEMU overhead.
strengths: Security-first architecture (empty allowlist denies all, encrypted secrets)
Clean compilation with strict style checks
weaknesses: No native ARM64 binary (GNAT Community lacks arm64 support)
Startup latency higher than Rust/Zig competitors (even accounting for QEMU)
Feature parity gap: 9 channels vs 17 (competitors), 5 providers vs 12-22
Throughput limited by single-threaded Ada tasking model
next_actions: Run benchmarks on native x86_64 CI for fair comparison
Close feature parity gap on channels and providers
Investigate GNAT Pro or GCC 14 for ARM64 native builds
Profile and optimize startup path (reduce config loading overhead)
